explanation: The body is dense with self-references (Acme as a sentence subject in nearly every sentence and section lead), editorial commentary about strategy and impact, and corporate jargon. The page header already shows the org name, so 'Acme'-as-subject buries each section's actual news. Every claim is also a vague hand-wave — no specific feature names, version numbers, or release details — so the body would also fail faithfulness if inputs were supplied.

criteria.name: No org-name self-reference as a sentence subject
criteria.evidence: Opens with 'Acme's recent work...' and uses 'Acme' / 'Acme team' / 'Acme Cloud' / 'Acme's observability suite' as the subject across every section.

criteria.name: No editorializing about strategy or impact
criteria.evidence: 'further improved', 'pushing forward', 'transformative new SDK', 'leap forward', 'doubled down', 'clear edge over alternatives'.

criteria.name: Prefer plain language; avoid corporate jargon
criteria.evidence: 'leverage cutting-edge', 'world-class developer experiences', 'seamless integrations', 'next-generation workloads', 'utilize advanced storage features', 'comprehensive rethinking', 'best-in-class performance'.

criteria.name: No vague hand-waves; concrete shipped items
criteria.evidence: 'powerful new capabilities', 'advanced storage features', 'comprehensive rethinking of the developer experience' — no specific features, versions, or release names anywhere.
